title: Andrej Karpathy says he likes to ramble at his LLM
excerpt: Karpathy, a leading AI researcher, said he will send LLMs 10-minute long "total mess" memos — and calls the bots response "useful."
content_html:
Andrej Karpathy’s trick for getting better answers from AI: Just ramble at it for 10 minutes

By Ben Shimkus

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Andrej Karpathy has some advice for AI users trying to write beautifully crafted prompts.

Stop. Instead, you should just ramble. On, and on, and on.

In a Tuesday post on X, Karpathy — a leading AI researcher at Anthropic who coined the term "vibe-coding" — said he sometimes turns on voice mode and prattles at a large language model for about 10 minutes.

He called the sessions a "total mess, anything goes, full stream of consciousness."

"Sometimes the LLM needs more bits to understand what you're trying to achieve," he wrote.

The approach helps properly divide responsibilities between the human and the bot, he said: Speaking lets the human unload every half-formed thought about a project, while the chatbot reconstructs the monologue into something cleaner and more organized.

Karpathy said he often starts by issuing a warning to the chatbot — that his thought process might be jumbled and the text might contain typos.

"The result is that you improve the mind meld and have to correct things less from that point on," he said.

Other X users weren't fully sold on Karpathy's pitch to blather.

Karpathy's pitch also comes as Silicon Valley is sharpening its AI voice tools — and trying to get users to speak up.

Earlier this month, OpenAI unveiled GPT-Live, the new model that's powering ChatGPT Voice. The company's $230 mini-keyboard also has a voice-recording key that sends audio prompts directly to an agent. Anthropic also launched updates to its voice model on Thursday, allowing users to choose between its Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku models.

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Ben Shimkus is a reporter for the Business News desk. He writes about cars, transportation, retail, and jobs. Ben's reporting has appeared in Rolling Stone, The Verge, Automotive News, USA Today, AutoBody News, LGBTQ Nation, TopSpeed, and Out Magazine. He's also held staff writing positions at The U.S. Sun and the Daily Mail. He graduated from NYU with a Master's in journalism in 2024. Email Ben at bshimkus@insider.com or message him privately on Signal at bshimkus.41.

content_html_zh:
安德烈·卡帕蒂 (Andrej Karpathy) 从 AI 中获得更好答案的技巧：闲逛 10 分钟

作者：Ben Shimkus 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Andrej Karpathy 对试图编写精美提示的 AI 用户提出了一些建议。

停止。 相反，你应该只是闲逛。 继续，继续，继续。

在周二发布的 X 帖子中，Anthropic 的首席人工智能研究员、创造了“vibe-coding”一词的 Karpathy 表示，他有时会打开语音模式，对大型语言模型喋喋不休大约 10 分钟。

他称这些会议“一团糟，什么都发生，全是意识流”。

“有时法学硕士需要更多的知识来理解你想要实现的目标，”他写道。

他说，这种方法有助于正确划分人类和机器人之间的责任：说话可以让人类摆脱对项目的每一个半成形的想法，而聊天机器人则将独白重建为更清晰、更有组织的东西。

卡帕蒂说，他经常首先向聊天机器人发出警告——他的思维过程可能很混乱，文本可能包含拼写错误。

“结果是你的思维融合得到了改善，从那时起就必须更少地纠正事情，”他说。

其他 X 用户并没有完全接受 Karpathy 的胡言乱语。

Karpathy 的推介之际，硅谷正在完善其人工智能语音工具，并试图让用户畅所欲言。

本月早些时候，OpenAI 推出了 GPT-Live，这是为 ChatGPT Voice 提供支持的新模型。 该公司售价 230 美元的迷你键盘还配有录音键，可以直接向客服人员发送音频提示。 Anthropic 还在周四推出了其语音模型的更新，允许用户在 Opus、Sonnet 和 Haiku 模型之间进行选择。

阅读下一篇

本·希姆库斯 (Ben Shimkus) 是《商业新闻》台的记者。 他撰写有关汽车、交通、零售和就业的文章。 Ben 的报道曾发表在《滚石》、《The Verge》、《汽车新闻》、《今日美国》、《AutoBody News》、《LGBTQ Nation》、《TopSpeed》和《Out》杂志上。 他还曾在《美国太阳报》和《每日邮报》担任撰稿职务。 他于 2024 年从纽约大学毕业，获得新闻学硕士学位。 给 Ben 发电子邮件 bshimkus@insider.com 或在 Signal 上私下给他发消息 bshimkus.41。
